title: Log message template should be syntactically correct
description: Log message template should be syntactically correctAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugloggingAmessage templatemust conform to the specification. The rule raises an issue if the template string violates the template string grammar.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoA message template needs to comply with a set of rules.Logging providerparse the template and enrich log entries with the information found in the template. An unparsable message template leads to corrupted log entries and might result in a loss of information in the logs.The rule covers the following logging frameworks:Nuget package -SerilogNuget package -NlogNuget package -Microsoft.Extensions.LoggingAvailable In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Service
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
// Noncompliant: Invalid placeholder syntax
logger.LogError("User {Name logged in", userName); // Noncompliant - missing closing brace

// Noncompliant: Empty placeholder
logger.LogInformation("Value: {}", value); // Noncompliant

// Noncompliant: Invalid characters in placeholder
logger.LogWarning("Error in {User-Name}", user); // Noncompliant - hyphen not allowed
compliant:
// Compliant: Valid placeholder syntax
logger.LogError("User {Name} logged in", userName); // Compliant

// Compliant: Named placeholder
logger.LogInformation("Value: {Value}", value); // Compliant

// Compliant: Valid identifier
logger.LogWarning("Error in {UserName}", user); // Compliant

checks.reasoning: Message templates must conform to specification for logging providers to parse them correctly
